methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: RC-RV partners with ed2go to launch innovative, on-demand workforce training programs - Roanoke College
signals.signal_reading: Roanoke College's partnership with ed2go to deliver on-demand workforce training signals distributed capacity-building infrastructure in a traditionally underserved region, creating pathways for peer-to-peer skill development beyond metro centers.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure specifically on Richmond's young workforce indicates the capital region is experiencing concentrated adoption dynamics where AI capability becomes a competitive requirement, potentially deepening the skills gap between metro and rural Virginia.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's institutional partnership with AI Ready RVA represents deep, organized investment in AI education within the Richmond metro hub, building concentrated expertise rather than distributed capability across Virginia's regions.

signals.title: Roanoke College president: Virginia’s future workforce depends on fixing the path from college to career - Cardinal News
signals.signal_reading: Roanoke College president's focus on fixing the college-to-career pipeline signals awareness that distributed workforce development requires structural change, not just platform access—essential infrastructure for broad-based AI fluency outside NoVA.

signals.title: Doug Hoven on How AI Is Changing the Way Homes Are Bought and Sold in Hampton Roads - The Ritz Herald
signals.signal_reading: AI adoption in Hampton Roads real estate represents shallow, platform-delivered automation in a non-tech-hub region—widespread tool use without deep technical capability, exemplifying distributed but surface-level AI integration.
